idea.hypothesis: When a price sits near the top of its recent (1-month) range but still near the bottom of its longer (6-month) range, it is a structural laggard in the early innings of a recovery thrust; the spread between two rolling percentile-rank horizons isolates that transition before single-horizon measures register it. Above the 200-day MA this divergence resolves upward as the laggard catches up; below it, the mirror configuration (locally weak, structurally strong) marks a faltering leader rolling over. Markets underreact to the moment a regime-aligned name shifts timescale leadership.
idea.novelty_justification: Every prior idea uses a single-horizon percentile rank (of drawdown depth, distance-from-MA z-score, streak length, autocorrelation, %B, gap autocorrelation, etc.). This one's mechanism is the SPREAD between two rolling percentile ranks of raw price computed over different lookback windows - a cross-timescale rank divergence. No prior idea contrasts a short-window rank against a long-window rank of the same series as the entry trigger; the signal primitive is the gap, not any single rank.
idea.failure_mode: The entry conditions may be near-contradictory in practice - requiring price above the 200-day MA yet near its 6-month low simultaneously - so trades fire rarely and mostly at false-dawn bounces that fail, while the breakeven-then-ATR-trail exit whipsaws the position out before the multi-week recovery the hypothesis depends on can complete.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Long the two-bar-confirmed upside release of a high inside-bar-fraction compression coil, ATR rolling-high trailing stop.
idea.hypothesis: When a sustained share of recent daily bars are 'inside bars' (high and low both contained within the prior bar's range), price action is coiling into a tightly nested range rather than merely shrinking monotonically. Such containment reflects a temporary balance between buyers and sellers that tends to resolve directionally; an upside resolution confirmed by consecutive up-closes carries enough order-flow imbalance to trend for one to two weeks before mean-reverting.
idea.novelty_justification: The compression primitive is the rolling fraction of inside bars (nested-range containment), percentile-ranked against its own history. Prior ideas detect compression via consecutive contraction streaks, true-range queue capacity ceilings, return-dispersion convoys, or ATR coefficient-of-variation flatlining. Inside-bar nesting is a categorically different measurement: it counts bars fully engulfed by their predecessor rather than measuring whether range magnitude is shrinking, so a market can score high here even with stable (non-decreasing) range size.
idea.failure_mode: Inside bars are relatively uncommon on daily SPY, so the inside-bar fraction may sit at a low, near-constant level for long stretches; its percentile rank then becomes noisy and the top-percentile gate can trigger on statistically meaningless fluctuations, producing few trades and an unstable, overfit signal.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when distance-from-MA z-score crosses its yield point into the plastic regime; hold until the stretch relaxes
idea.hypothesis: A price stretch above its short moving average behaves like material deformation: small stretches are elastic and snap back (mean reversion), but a stretch that crosses a yield threshold becomes plastic - a permanent regime change where the trend has genuinely strengthened rather than overextended. Once the yield point is breached, continuation is more likely than reversion until the deformation fully relaxes back toward the average. The market inefficiency exploited is that traders reflexively fade large positive z-scores, underpricing the subset of stretches that represent real trend acceleration.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing z-score entries in the list buy negative z-score DIPS (bottom-percentile, velocity-release of deep negative z) or trade z-trend direction with ATR rolling-high trailing stops plus a refractory lockout. This strategy instead BUYS high positive z-score - betting the stretch is plastic, not elastic - and uses no ATR, no percentile rank, and no trailing/time stop. Its distinguishing mechanism is a latched plastic-state: entry only when z crosses up through a yield point, then a path-dependent hold until z relaxes back below a release level (signal-reversal exit on the dissolution of the stretch). The elastic-vs-plastic yield-point latch is the core mechanic, not a parameter tweak of any listed idea.
idea.failure_mode: Large positive distance-from-MA z-scores are historically among the best mean-reversion short signals on equity indices. If the elastic/plastic distinction does not actually exist - i.e. stretches beyond the yield point still revert just as often - the strategy systematically buys local tops and the relaxation exit hands back most of the move as z decays, producing a negative-edge drag worsened by commission and slippage on frequent 1-2 day trades.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Buy consecutive down-close streaks as dissipating traffic shockwaves; size by streak depth; exit on up-close streak reversal
idea.hypothesis: A run of consecutive down closes behaves like a traffic congestion shockwave: selling pressure bunches up bar after bar, and once each successive drop gets shallower the wave is dissipating and a short-horizon bounce is likely. Retail and momentum sellers exhaust their flow over a few days, and the deeper the streak the sharper the snap-back. The reversion completes within one to two days, so holding only until buying re-establishes itself (an up-close streak) captures the bounce without overstaying.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas use streaks only for momentum continuation (same-sign return streak past a percentile yield point) or for range-contraction coils. This one uses the raw consecutive down-CLOSE count as a mean-reversion congestion gauge with an explicit shockwave-deceleration filter (each drop shallower than the last), and the exit is a pure opposite-streak reversal (an up-close streak), not a stop, percentile, time, or ATR exit. Position size is scaled directly by streak depth rather than by z-score, ATR, or volatility. No moving average, z-score, ATR, percentile rank, or autocorrelation appears anywhere.
idea.failure_mode: In sustained downtrends, deep down-close streaks recur and the long-only, filter-free entry repeatedly buys falling knives; because the exit only fires on a two-up-close streak, the position can be held through a large drawdown while waiting for the reversal condition that never cleanly arrives.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)

idea.one_line_summary: Buy the two-bar rising turn after price spent a long consecutive streak submerged below a tide-level MA
idea.hypothesis: When price drifts and stays below its medium-term moving average (the 'mean water level') for many consecutive bars, sellers progressively exhaust and the asset becomes oversold relative to its own equilibrium. Like an outgoing tide that must return, a sustained submerged streak followed by the first clear two-bar upturn marks the turning point where reversion back toward the tide level pays in the next 1-2 days.
idea.novelty_justification: The streak primitive here is consecutive bars CLOSING BELOW A MOVING AVERAGE (submersion depth in bar-count), not consecutive down-closes (#30) nor same-sign return streaks (#23). Price can rise and fall within the streak as long as it stays under the tide line, so this measures duration-of-depression rather than directional momentum. Entry additionally requires a two-bar up-close confirmation read from the bar BEFORE confirmation, which is a distinct trigger from %B band crossings (#21) or distance-from-MA z-score yield points (#22/#29).
idea.failure_mode: In a sustained downtrend the price can stay below the tide MA indefinitely and produce repeated false two-bar bounces; even with the 200MA regime gate, a slow grind down keeps close above the 200MA while the tide MA keeps getting pierced, generating losing entries that the fixed ATR stop only partially contains.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Trade volume-confirmed, SNR-filtered jumps in close's relative position within its range, percentile-gated, fixed ATR vol-stop
idea.hypothesis: When the close abruptly shifts its location inside the recent high-low range and that shift is large relative to its own recent noise AND backed by unusually heavy volume, informed participants have repriced the asset rather than noise traders churning. Such volume-confirmed positional repricings tend to extend for a bar or two before mean-reverting. Filtering by signal-to-noise removes the many small range-position wobbles that carry no edge.
idea.novelty_justification: The list trades range-position levels (%B band crosses, envelope-midpoint position), range-position autocorrelation, or range-band displacement speed. None trade the one-bar VELOCITY of relative-position scaled by its own noise (a signal-to-noise ratio) and require that ratio to rank in a top percentile while a separate volume percentile rank confirms it. The mechanism is a volume-gated SNR threshold on positional change, not a level cross, not autocorrelation, not coil release.
idea.failure_mode: Volume spikes on SPY frequently coincide with index rebalances, options expiry, and macro shocks where the volume-confirmed positional jump is the start of a multi-day trend or a violent reversal, not a clean 1-2 day extension. The fixed ATR stop using entry-bar ATR may be too tight during the volatility expansion that the volume surge itself signals, stopping out winners and losers alike.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when return-dispersion coil compresses AND close-to-close return efficiency ratio confirms coherent upward drift; fixed ATR vol-stop.
idea.hypothesis: When the dispersion of close-to-close returns compresses into a tight coil, the eventual move tends to be directional, but a coil alone gives no edge on direction. Pairing the coil with a return-path efficiency ratio (net displacement over gross path length) filters out coils that are merely choppy noise and isolates the ones where small returns are already drifting coherently upward. The market under-prices the continuation of these quiet, low-noise upward drifts because they lack the volume and volatility that draw attention.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing compression ideas either release on a single up-return, use percentile capacity ceilings, coefficient-of-variation flatlines, or inside-bar fractions, and pair with fixed-bar or trailing exits. This strategy's distinguishing mechanism is a two-primitive AND between (a) a rolling percentile rank of return-dispersion compression and (b) a Kaufman-style efficiency ratio of the close-to-close return path used as an explicit signal-to-noise gate with a positive-net-displacement requirement. No prior idea uses path-efficiency as the second independent agreeing primitive, and the exit is a fixed (non-trailing) ATR vol-stop, distinct from the trailing/fixed-bar exits in the convoy and capacity-queue ideas.
idea.failure_mode: The efficiency ratio and dispersion compression are not truly independent — a tight coil mechanically inflates the efficiency ratio when even a small net drift exists — so the AND may collapse into one primitive, producing correlated false positives and entries right before the coil breaks downward, where the fixed vol-stop locks in a loss within one or two bars.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)

idea.one_line_summary: Trade the expansion bar that breaks a percentile-compressed high-low channel; candle direction picks side, fixed ATR vol-stop.
idea.hypothesis: When a market's high-low channel width sits in the bottom quartile of its recent distribution, accumulated order flow has nowhere to go and the next sharp single-bar range expansion tends to be a genuine directional release rather than noise. The sign of that expansion bar's close-to-close move reveals which way the pent-up imbalance discharged, and that impulse persists for roughly one to two days before mean-reverting.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing coil/compression entries measure compression via inside-bar fraction, return dispersion, ATR coefficient-of-variation, or true-range queue capacity. This strategy measures compression purely geometrically as the rolling percentile rank of channel WIDTH (rolling-max-high minus rolling-min-low), and uses a SECOND, independent rolling percentile rank — of the single bar's true range — as the release trigger. The arming gate and the trigger are two distinct rank series; direction comes from the breakout bar's body sign, not from a z-score, autocorrelation, MA distance, or streak. No list item combines width-rank arming with range-rank release.
idea.failure_mode: Compressed-channel breakouts whipsaw frequently: the ATR used for the stop is itself measured during the low-volatility coil, so it is small, the k*ATR stop is tight, and the position is likely to be stopped out or hit the 2-bar time cap before any sustained move develops — producing many small losses that overwhelm the occasional clean release.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Long when consecutive-bars-above-EMA streak crosses its own rolling percentile threshold; profit-target or time-stop exit with refractory lockout.
idea.hypothesis: When price has held above its trend EMA for an unusually long unbroken run relative to its own recent history, the market is demonstrating regime-level persistence rather than transient momentum. Such persistence tends to continue over multi-week horizons because trend-following capital reinforces an established regime. The bet is that an above-trend dwell-time streak reaching a high percentile of its trailing distribution flags a self-sustaining uptrend worth riding for 3-4 weeks.
idea.novelty_justification: The closest prior idea trades a same-sign return streak (consecutive up vs down closes) crossing a percentile with a flip-exit. This strategy's streak primitive is fundamentally different: it counts consecutive bars price stays above its trend EMA — a dwell-time / regime-persistence measure that survives individual down bars and resets only on a true trend break, whereas a return streak resets on any single opposite bar. It measures how long a regime has lasted, not micro-momentum. Entry is a crossover of a dynamic rolling-quantile threshold (not merely 'exceeds'), the exit is profit-target plus hard time-stop (not a flip), and a refractory lockout suppresses immediate re-entry whipsaw.
idea.failure_mode: A long above-EMA dwell streak may flag trend exhaustion rather than continuation — by the time the streak hits its 80th percentile the easy gains are gone, and the strategy buys near local tops, with the time-stop forcing exits into mean-reverting pullbacks before any trend resumes.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Fade accumulated overnight down-gap clusters via a Schmitt-trigger gap-deficit state machine; two-bar-confirmed entry, signal-reversal exit.
idea.hypothesis: Overnight gaps in an index ETF reflect non-trading-hours order imbalance that intraday liquidity providers partially repair. A short run of consistently negative overnight gaps accumulates an 'overnight deficit' that tends to overshoot fair value, so the next several sessions drift up as the deficit is closed. A single gap is noise; a rolling cluster of them is a measurable, fadeable dislocation.
idea.novelty_justification: The mechanism is a cumulative overnight-return deficit accumulator (rolling SUM of gaps), not the lag-1 autocorrelation of the gap series used by the existing Schmitt-trigger gap idea. It is not drawdown-conditional (no drawdown variable, no fade-vs-follow flip) and not calendar-based. The hysteresis here gates a single state — 'stretched-down' — defined purely on accumulated overnight pressure, distinct from every distance-from-MA, %B, channel, or autocorrelation construct in the list.
idea.failure_mode: SPY overnight gaps carry a genuine risk premium and a cluster of down-gaps often marks the start of a real selloff rather than a mean-reverting dislocation. Because the only exit is signal-reversal (z recovering above exit_z) with no stop-loss, in a sustained crash the strategy buys the deficit early and holds while it deepens, since z never recovers until the bottom.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Long breakout above a trailing close-price percentile level, confirmed by positive ROC acceleration, post-exit refractory lockout, breakeven-then-trail exit.
idea.hypothesis: When price closes above the high-q percentile of its own recent close distribution, it is escaping the bulk of recent value-area trading, and if the rate-of-change is simultaneously accelerating the escape is being fueled by fresh demand rather than mean drift. Such percentile escapes accompanied by upward ROC convexity tend to extend for several weeks before the buying impulse exhausts.
idea.novelty_justification: The breakout level is not a rolling max, a channel width percentile, or a cumulative-return high - it is the q-th percentile of the raw trailing close-price distribution, so the strategy buys an escape from the value area rather than a new extreme. Confirmation uses the sign of smoothed ROC acceleration directly (not its autocorrelation, not a compression coil, not a two-bar streak). Combined with a post-exit refractory lockout and breakeven-then-trail management, no listed idea shares this mechanism.
idea.failure_mode: In a sustained strong uptrend the q-percentile of trailing closes lags far below current price, so `close > level` is almost always true and the percentile filter degenerates into a no-op - the entry gate collapses to acceleration alone, which is noisy and whipsaw-prone.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Long SPY when the rolling variance ratio of close-to-close returns signals a positive-autocorrelation regime above the 200-day MA.
idea.hypothesis: When daily returns are positively autocorrelated, single-period variance compounds super-linearly across horizons and the variance ratio (q-period return variance divided by q times one-period variance) rises above 1. Such persistence regimes cluster: once returns begin 'infecting' their successors with the same sign, the trend tends to continue for several weeks. SPY is most likely to display this exploitable persistence while structurally in an uptrend (above its 200-day MA), so a long-only bet on the persistence regime should capture multi-week drift.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior autocorrelation ideas estimate a rolling lag-1 autocorrelation COEFFICIENT (of ROC-acceleration, of the gap series) and percentile-rank it. This strategy uses the Lo-MacKinlay VARIANCE RATIO instead - a multi-horizon aggregate autocorrelation statistic comparing q-period to 1-period return dispersion. It is not a lag-1 estimator, not percentile-ranked, and built directly on close-to-close returns rather than gaps or acceleration. The regime is defined by VR crossing an absolute threshold near 1, a structurally different detector.
idea.failure_mode: SPY daily-return autocorrelation is weak and unstable; the overlapping variance ratio is noisy, so a threshold near 1.0 can whipsaw and the regime flag may flip the entry condition before a 3-4 week hold completes. Worse, VR tends to peak late in a trend, so entries may cluster near exhaustion points.

slots.exit_rule: fixed-bar exit (exit exactly N bars after entry, no signal-based exit)

idea.one_line_summary: Trade the sign of upside-vs-downside RMS semi-deviation asymmetry as a trend-strength gauge; fixed-bar exit.
idea.hypothesis: During genuine trends, realized volatility is asymmetric: in uptrends the dispersion of up-moves structurally exceeds the dispersion of down-moves, and vice versa. This volatility-skew imbalance is a cleaner trend-strength reading than price direction alone because it filters out symmetric chop where up and down dispersion are balanced. Persistent asymmetry tends to outlast the bar it appears on, giving a 1-2 week edge.
idea.novelty_justification: No prior idea decomposes volatility into upside vs downside RMS semi-deviation and trades the normalized asymmetry between them. The list uses total dispersion coils, ATR coefficient-of-variation, distance-from-MA z-scores, gap autocorrelation, and streak counts - all of which collapse direction and magnitude or use undirected volatility. Here the signal IS the directional imbalance of second-moment dispersion, not of price or of total vol.
idea.failure_mode: Upside and downside semi-deviation are both inflated by the same volatility clustering, so the asymmetry can degenerate into a noisy, lagging proxy for the recent return sign - effectively a short-window momentum strategy that whipsaws at trend turns, and the mandatory fixed-bar exit prevents cutting a wrong-way entry early.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Buy the ATR up-tick release that ends a consecutive-ATR-decline compression streak, in a bull regime, vol-targeted size
idea.hypothesis: Volatility decays in a near-monotonic streak as a market 'jam' compresses, then clears in a shockwave: the first bar where ATR ticks back up is the release of stored potential. In an established uptrend (above the 200-day MA), that volatility-expansion release tends to resolve upward, so entering on the up-close release bar after a long ATR-decline streak captures the front edge of the shockwave. Position size is scaled inversely to realized volatility so each trade carries comparable risk.
idea.novelty_justification: The streak primitive counts consecutive DECLINES IN ATR (a volatility-direction streak), not consecutive up/down closes, above/below-MA bars, or same-sign returns used everywhere in the list. Entry fires specifically on the ATR up-tick that breaks the streak — the shockwave release bar — rather than on price thrust, percentile crossings, or a CV-of-ATR flatline coil. It is a volatility-decay-streak-then-release mechanism, distinct from every price/return/gap streak idea generated so far.
idea.failure_mode: In sustained bull regimes ATR is usually already low and drifting, so a clean multi-bar monotonic ATR-decline streak rarely co-occurs with the regime filter — entries are sparse, making per-fold WFO Sharpe noisy and unstable. When the streak does break, the volatility expansion resolves downward roughly as often as upward, so the up-close confirmation may not be enough edge after costs.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)

idea.one_line_summary: Continuously long/short the sign of smoothed ROC acceleration as a predator-prey cycle phase oscillator, vol-targeted size, breakeven-then-trail exit.
idea.hypothesis: SPY momentum and volatility behave like a Lotka-Volterra predator-prey system: momentum (prey) builds, then volatility (predator) surges and consumes it, then volatility decays and momentum rebuilds. Rate-of-change acceleration - the second derivative of price - leads the momentum level by roughly a quarter cycle, so the sign of smoothed acceleration tells you which growth/decay phase of the cycle the market currently occupies. Riding that phase signal two-sided should harvest the cyclical alternation while volatility-targeting keeps risk constant across the loud and quiet halves of the cycle.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ROC-acceleration ideas use it as an event trigger gated by compression or breakouts (compression-gated two-bar-confirmed entry; breakout confirmed by positive acceleration). This strategy instead treats the sign of smoothed acceleration as a permanent two-sided phase oscillator - the portfolio is continuously invested long or short, flipping with the predator-prey cycle rather than waiting for a discrete entry event. No gate, no breakout, no streak, no percentile rank: the mechanism is the phase-position of the second derivative itself, with volatility-targeting as the structural core.
idea.failure_mode: The second difference of price is extremely noisy; even with a 3-bar EMA the acceleration sign whipsaws frequently, churning commissions and reversing positions before the +3% favorable move ever arms the breakeven stop - so the trailing-exit machinery rarely engages and the strategy degrades into noise-trading drag.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Long-only regime switch on the recency ordering of rolling range extremes (Aroon-style), profit-target or time-stop exit.
idea.hypothesis: When the most recent extreme in a stock's high-low range history flips from being a fresh low to being a fresh new high, the market's directional 'regime' has switched and a multi-day continuation tends to follow. SPY exhibits short-run autocorrelation after such regime fronts pass, so the moment the newest high overtakes the newest low in recency is an exploitable entry. The edge decays within a couple of weeks, motivating a fixed profit-target or time-stop exit.
idea.novelty_justification: No prior idea classifies regime by the temporal recency of range extremes. This computes bars-since-rolling-high versus bars-since-rolling-low (an Aroon-style construct) and treats the crossover of those recencies as a propagating regime front. Existing ideas use distance-from-MA z-scores, percentile ranks, compression coils, return streaks, ROC acceleration, variance ratios or semi-deviation asymmetry — none keys off WHICH extreme happened more recently. The 'shockwave front speed' idea uses midpoint-displacement/churn ratio (a magnitude measure), not extreme recency ordering.
idea.failure_mode: Aroon recency flips are lagging and whippy in sideways markets — a fresh-high recency advantage can decay within days, so the fresh-flip entry repeatedly buys just before mean reversion and the 10-bar time-stop closes most trades near flat, bleeding commission and slippage with no net edge.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Trade gaps whose magnitude breaks out of its own rolling percentile band, confirmed by an intraday-hold close, SNR-filtered.
idea.hypothesis: On most days the overnight gap is small noise drawn from a tight recent distribution; occasionally a gap is large enough to rank in the extreme upper percentile of its own recent magnitude distribution, signalling a genuine information shock rather than microstructure noise. When such an outsized gap is also 'held' to the close (the bar does not fill back through its open), the same-day participants have ratified the new level, and the framework predicts a short multi-day continuation drift in the gap's direction.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap ideas operate on accumulated gap state: the Schmitt-trigger fades a cumulative down-gap deficit, the predator-prey idea trades rolling up/down gap COUNTS, and the calendar idea uses a first-of-month gap as a seasonal omen. This strategy treats the single-event gap MAGNITUDE itself as the series that breaks out — it enters only when one day's |gap| ranks above a percentile of the recent |gap| distribution, then requires same-bar intraday-hold confirmation (close not retracing past open) and an SNR gate against ATR noise. No prior idea uses gap-magnitude percentile rank plus close-vs-open hold confirmation as the breakout trigger.
idea.failure_mode: Index ETF gaps overwhelmingly mean-revert — gaps get filled — so a continuation bet is fighting the dominant tendency; the percentile gate also yields few trades, and the close-vs-open hold filter selects bars that have already run, so entries are late and the 3-5 day drift may be exhausted before the fill.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Staircase breakout: count closes beyond prior bar's full range; enter when streak exceeds rolling percentile, flip on opposite
idea.hypothesis: When SPY closes beyond the entire prior bar's high-low range several bars in a row, it forms a 'staircase' of range breakouts that signals an order-flow imbalance strong enough to overwhelm intrabar liquidity, not just close-to-close drift. Such staircases tend to persist for one to two weeks because institutional rebalancing flows are sticky. The streak length needed to be meaningful is regime-dependent, so a fixed count misfires - the breakout only matters when the streak is unusually long relative to its own recent history.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior streak ideas count down-closes, bars-above-EMA, same-sign returns, or bars-below-MA - all close-to-close or close-vs-line comparisons. This counts bars whose close clears the PRIOR bar's entire high-low range (a per-bar range breakout 'rung'), incorporating intrabar extremes into the streak primitive itself. The mechanism is a staircase of nested range breakouts, and the exit is a true signal-reversal flip driven by the opposite-side streak crossing its own percentile - distinct from Aroon recency, channel breakouts, or fixed/ATR-trailing exits.
idea.failure_mode: Closing beyond the prior bar's full range is rare, so qualifying staircases are infrequent and the rolling percentile of a mostly-zero streak series often collapses to the min_streak floor, making the percentile twist inert and entries sparse - leaving long flat stretches and a tiny, high-variance trade sample that walk-forward optimization cannot stabilize.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when rolling Spearman rank-correlation of close vs time confirms a strong monotonic uptrend; ATR rolling-high trailing exit.
idea.hypothesis: A genuinely strong trend is monotonic, not merely high-returning: prices climb in rank order with time. The Spearman rank-correlation between close and the time index measures exactly this monotonicity and is robust to outlier bars that distort return- or slope-based trend gauges. When rank-monotonicity is high, near-term continuation is likelier than reversal, so a 1-2 day momentum hold captures the persistence.
idea.novelty_justification: No prior idea uses rank correlation of price against time. The signal primitive is the Spearman coefficient (1 - 6*Sum d^2 / (n(n^2-1))) computed as the concordance of close ranks with the fixed time-rank vector - a pure rank-order monotonicity measure, not a percentile threshold on the close itself, not a return/streak count, not a slope or z-score. It scores HOW ordered the climb is rather than how big or how recent it is.
idea.failure_mode: Spearman trend strength is smooth and lagging; by the time it crosses the entry threshold the trend is often mature, so entries cluster near local tops and the ATR trailing stop whipsaws out for small losses while the rank measure stays elevated, causing repeated re-entries into the same exhausted move.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Long/short the recovery-thrust-to-drawdown-depth ratio (escape velocity per unit hole), two-bar confirmed, reversal-exit
idea.hypothesis: When SPY climbs out of a drawdown, the speed of the climb relative to the depth of the hole it is climbing out of carries information: a fast recoupment of a deep loss (high escape velocity) reflects genuine buyer conviction and tends to continue, while continued sinking inside an already-deep hole (negative escape velocity) reflects a failed bounce and tends to extend. Markets under-react to the *normalized* recovery rate because participants anchor on absolute price levels and the prior peak rather than on how efficiently lost ground is being regained.
idea.novelty_justification: No prior idea forms the scalar thrust/|drawdown-depth-R-bars-ago| — recovery return normalized by the depth of the hole it must escape. Existing drawdown ideas use distance-from-MA z-scores, consecutive-streak-below-MA counts, gap-count predator-prey cycles, or percentile-rank recovery from a trough. This instead measures an 'escape velocity per unit hole depth' and lets the sign of that single scalar pick long vs short within the same drawdown regime, with a reversal-only exit tied to that condition.
idea.failure_mode: The recovery_ratio is numerically fragile near the dd_floor — shallow drawdowns make the denominator tiny and inflate the ratio, producing noisy entries — and genuine deep-V recoveries are rare, so the long side may fire seldom while the negative-ratio 'relapse' short side dominates during bear regimes, yielding an asymmetric, regime-dependent equity curve rather than a balanced edge.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Wald-Wolfowitz runs-test detects a positive-autocorrelation regime; enter on a live up-close streak above 200MA.
idea.hypothesis: Daily SPY returns alternate between random behavior and episodes of genuine serial persistence. When the Wald-Wolfowitz runs test shows significantly fewer sign-runs than a coin-flip null would produce, returns are clustering - an up-close streak is more likely to extend than to revert. Trading these statistically-confirmed persistence windows, only in a bull regime, should capture short multi-day continuations.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior streak ideas use raw streak length, streak percentiles, or streak-below-MA turns; the prior autocorrelation idea uses a variance ratio. This strategy uses the Wald-Wolfowitz runs test - it counts TOTAL runs in a window and z-scores that count against the analytic random-walk mean/variance of the run count. The runs-z is a hypothesis test against a coin-flip null, a different statistic from variance ratio or percentile rank, and it is used purely as a regime gate while the live consecutive up-streak count is the entry trigger.
idea.failure_mode: Daily SPY serial dependence is weak and unstable; the runs-z may rarely cross a meaningful negative threshold, or cross it from noise on a short window, leaving few trades or trades with no real edge. The fixed profit-target can also cap genuine continuations short while the time-stop dumps slow winners.

slots.exit_rule: fixed-bar exit (exit exactly N bars after entry, no signal-based exit)

idea.one_line_summary: Buy persistent consecutive up-gap streaks with deadband hysteresis; streak-scaled size; fixed-bar exit.
idea.hypothesis: A run of consecutive overnight up-gaps reflects sustained, repeated accumulation by informed buyers willing to pay up at the open day after day. This overnight demand persistence has short-horizon positive autocorrelation that tends to continue for roughly one to two weeks before mean-reverting, so entering on a confirmed up-gap streak captures the tail of that drift.
idea.novelty_justification: The 'predator-prey gap counts' idea uses rolling window counts and percentile-rank trough recovery; the 'Schmitt-trigger gap-deficit' idea fades down-gap clusters; the 'gap magnitude percentile band' idea trades single-gap size outliers. This one counts a true consecutive same-sign streak that resets only on an opposing gap, uses a symmetric deadband so tiny gaps neither extend nor reset the streak (hysteresis), trades up-gap momentum continuation rather than fading, and exits purely on bar count. Mechanism = streak persistence of gap sign, not rolling counts, percentiles, or state-machine fading.
idea.failure_mode: Consecutive up-gap streaks of length 3+ are rare and often cluster precisely at euphoric local tops, so the fixed-bar hold may systematically buy exhaustion and ride the subsequent reversion down with no signal-based escape hatch.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Long unfilled up-gaps that survive intraday on top-percentile volume; fixed ATR vol-stop, 5-day time cap
idea.hypothesis: An up-gap whose intraday low never trades back down to the prior close is a structurally 'unfilled' gap - sellers got no chance to fade it. When that survival coincides with volume in the top percentile of its trailing window, it signals genuine capacity-overflow demand: more buy interest arrived than the day could absorb, and the residual demand spills into the following days. Such gaps tend to see continuation over a 3-5 day window rather than reversion.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior gap ideas key on gap magnitude percentile breakouts, rolling up/down gap COUNTS (predator-prey), consecutive-gap STREAKS with hysteresis, or fading down-gap CLUSTERS via a Schmitt trigger. This strategy uses neither magnitude, count, nor streak: its core primitive is intraday gap SURVIVAL - the structural condition low >= prior_close meaning the gap was never filled all day - with the percentile threshold applied to VOLUME (a capacity-overflow trigger), not to the gap itself. No listed idea conditions on the gap remaining unfilled for the entire session.
idea.failure_mode: Unfilled high-volume up-gaps are highly visible and may already be fully priced by the open, so gap-and-go frequently stalls into 3-5 day consolidation rather than continuation; in that chop the fixed ATR stop gets whipsawed out near the lows before any drift materializes.
